title: Device for decontaminating a region and verifying decontamination of the region
abstract: A self contained, 1 V to 550 V powered UVC device used to sterilize spaces like vehicle cabins or indoor rooms or spaces, while providing the ability to monitor both pre and post sterilization. While operating, the device would collect, on suitable gathering/testing media, both air that has been sterilized, and unsterilized air that passes through the media, allowing the media to be tested for the presence of both active pathogens that have not yet been killed from the device, as well as killed pathogens in the sterilized area. The UVC and media components may be integrated into one unit, or be powered and used both in combination, or as separate devices performing the sterilization and the sampling media. At the termination of UVC sterilization, additional different media has the now sterilized cabin or room air circulated through it, and with further media testing and evaluation, the efficiency/effectiveness of the sterilization process can be audited, and system operators can be advised.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. An assembly for treating and detecting contaminates in a region, the assembly comprising: a housing defining an internal volume and an opening providing fluid communication between the region and the internal volume; an ultraviolet light attached to the housing and configured to irradiate the region with UVC light to kill the contaminates in the region; and a first body attachable to the housing, wherein the first body includes: an internal cavity, an inlet opening extending through a wall of the first body for providing fluid communication between the region and the internal cavity, a fan disposed in the first body for drawing air from the region into the internal cavity through the inlet opening, and a filter membrane disposed proximate the opening and configured to remove contaminates from the air drawn into the internal cavity through the opening. 2. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the housing comprises: a fan configured to draw air from the region into the housing through the opening of the housing; and a filter membrane disposed proximate the opening of the housing and configured to remove contaminates from the air drawn into the housing. 3. The assembly according to claim 2, wherein the housing further comprises a holder attached to a wall of the housing for removably receiving the filter membrane of the housing therein. 4. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the housing comprises opposing ends and the fan of the housing is disposed at one of the opposing ends and is operable to draw the air into the housing. 5. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the first body is removably attachable to the housing. 6. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the first body comprises a power cable attachable to the housing for supplying power from the housing to the fan of the first body. 7. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the first body comprises a battery for supplying power to the fan of the first body. 8. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the assembly further comprises a second body attachable to the housing, wherein the second body includes: an internal cavity, an inlet opening extending through a wall of the second body for providing fluid communication between the region and the internal cavity, a fan disposed in the internal cavity of the second body for drawing air from the region into the internal cavity through the opening, and a filter membrane disposed proximate the opening of the second body and configured to remove contaminates from the air drawn into the internal cavity through the opening. 9. The assembly according to claim 1, wherein the assembly further comprises a control unit comprising logic to: activate the ultraviolet light; and activate the fan in the first body. 10. A collection device for removing contaminates from a region, the collection device comprising: a body including: an internal cavity, an inlet opening extending through a wall of the body for providing fluid communication between the region and the internal cavity, 

 an outlet opening extending through another wall of the body for providing fluid communication between the region and the internal cavity, a fan disposed in the body for generating an air stream the flows from the region, into the inlet opening, through the internal cavity, through the outlet opening and back to the region, and a filter membrane disposed in the air stream for removing contaminates from the air stream. 11. The collection device according to claim 10, wherein the body defines a slot dimensioned to removably receive the filter membrane therethrough. 12. The collection device according to claim 10, wherein the body includes an attachment element for removably attaching the body to a surface. 13. The collection device according to claim 10, further comprising a battery for supplying power to the fan. 14. The collection device according to claim 10, wherein the inlet opening and the outlet opening are formed in opposing walls of the body. 15. The collection device according to claim 10, wherein the collection device further comprises a power cable for supplying power from a power source to the fan. 16. A method of treating and detecting contaminates in an area, the method comprising: irradiating said area with UVC light from an ultraviolet light disposed in a first housing during a first time period, simultaneously drawing air through a filter membrane disposed in the first housing to capture contaminates thereon during the first time period, during a second time period after the first time period, drawing air through a second filter membrane disposed in a second housing, and 

 thereafter, analyzing the second filter membrane to determine a level of contaminates remaining in said area after said first time period. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the air is drawn through the second filter membrane via a fan disposed in the second housing. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the method further comprises: before the first time period, drawing air through a third filter membrane; and analyzing the third filter membrane to determine an initial level of contaminates in said area. 19. The method of claim 16, wherein the filter membrane disposed in the second housing has a size of 0.001 to 10 microns. 20. The method of claim 16, wherein the filter membrane disposed in the first housing has a size that is less than or equal to 0.3 microns.
